results.rendered_html: the star Sirius (so called because Śiva in the form of a hunter shot an arrow [represented by the three stars in the belt of Orion] at Brahmā transformed into a deer and pursuing his own daughter metamorphosed into a doe; cf. mṛga-vyādha),  Gaṇit. ;  Kathās.

results.rendered_html:
lubdhaka (von lubdha) m.
1〉 Jäger  AK. 2,10,21 .  H. 927 .  HALĀY. 2,441 .  MBH. 1,554 . 3,2395 . 5,1637 . 16,110 . 126 .  KĀM. NĪTIS. 16,7 .  Spr. 2234 . 2998 .  KATHĀS. 8,24. fg.  33,112 .  RĀJA-TAR. 5,345 .  BHĀG. P. 4,29,53 . 7,2,50 .  PAÑCAT. 104,15 . 106,6 . 7 .  HIT. 14,12 .
— 2〉 der Stern Sirius (vgl. mṛgavyādha)  GAṆITĀDHY.   BHAGRAHAY. 7 . 8 . Comm. zu  SŪRYAS. 8,10. fgg.   COLEBR. Misc. Ess. II, 352 . 464 . [Page6-0564] triśaṅkuḥ kiṃ na nīto dyāṃ viśvāmitreṇa lubdhakaḥ  KATHĀS. 28,88 .
— 3〉 bildliche Bez. des Afters  BHĀG. P. 4,25,53 . 29,15 .
— Vgl. ārdrā˚.
